$comment: contract-version 0.3.1 — daemons contribution semantics made first-class (skip-not-reject, sdk#51); 0.3.0 added the daemons capture (sdk#50); $id repointed from the archived molecule-contracts repo to this SSOT (fold: ai-sdk#41).
title: Marketplace Plugin Manifest Contract
description: SSOT JSON-Schema (draft 2020-12) for the Molecule marketplace plugin manifest — the publishable shape of a `plugin.yaml` artifact (molecule-ai-plugin-* repos), authored marketplace-side per RFC molecule-core#3285 (Tool-Contract SSOT & Codegen). DERIVED FROM THE REAL ARTIFACTS + their CI validator: molecule-ai-plugin-molecule-careful-bash/plugin.yaml, image-gen/plugin.yaml, gh-identity/plugin.yaml, molecule-hitl/plugin.yaml, validated by each plugin repo's `.molecule-ci/scripts/validate-plugin.py` (required: name/version/description; runtimes must be a list; content is one of SKILL.md/hooks/skills/rules). The design is VS-Code-shaped: `engines` pins the minimum host version (like engines.vscode), and `contributes` is the OPEN, forward-compatible contribution surface — its KNOWN keys (skills/hooks/rules/mcpServers/commands, v1) are validated by shape while UNKNOWN contribution points (e.g. future themes/tabs/canvasElements) are tolerated (additionalProperties:true at the contributes level), so a newer plugin never fails this schema on an additive contribution point. The top-level `skills`/`hooks`/`rules` string lists mirror the real plugin.yaml v0 shorthand and remain accepted alongside the richer `contributes`. CANONICAL RUNTIMES: the `runtimes` enum is the SSOT reconciliation of the cross-artifact drift — the canonical form is the HYPHEN spelling (`claude-code`, matching the workspace/org templates and validate-workspace-template.py's known set); the legacy plugin UNDERSCORE spelling (`claude_code`) used by today's plugin.yaml files is accepted as an alias (normalizes to the hyphen form). The enum is the SUPPORTED runtime set — claude-code, codex, hermes, openclaw, crewai, google-adk, external.

name.description: Plugin name (required by validate-plugin.py). The marketplace slug is derived from this. E.g. `molecule-careful-bash`, `image-gen`, `molecule-gh-identity`.

version.description: Plugin version (required). validate-plugin.py rejects any character outside `0-9.` — i.e. a dotted numeric version like `1.0.1` (no pre-release/build suffix). Modelled here with a numeric-dotted pattern mirroring that check.

publisher.description: Optional marketplace publisher/namespace (VS-Code `publisher`). Distinct from `author` (free-text attribution). Falls back to the org when absent.

author.description: Optional free-text author attribution as it appears in plugin.yaml (e.g. `Molecule AI`).

kind.description: Optional v1 plugin kind discriminator from plugin.yaml (e.g. `env-mutator` for the Go env-mutator class — gh-identity). Absent for skill/hook-class plugins. Open string (not enum) so new kinds are additive.

entrypoint.description: Optional Go entrypoint for code-class plugins (e.g. `pluginloader.BuildRegistry` — gh-identity). Absent for content-only plugins.

engines.description: Host-version constraints (VS-Code `engines`). Pins the minimum Molecule host the plugin requires.

runtimes.description: Runtimes this plugin supports (plugin.yaml `runtimes`; validate-plugin.py requires a list). Items use the CANONICAL runtime enum (see schema description): hyphen form canonical, underscore form accepted as alias.

skills.description: v0 shorthand: bare skill names contributed by the plugin (plugin.yaml `skills: [careful-mode]`). The richer structured form is `contributes.skills`.

hooks.description: v0 shorthand: bare hook names contributed by the plugin (plugin.yaml `hooks: [pre-bash-careful]`). The richer structured form is `contributes.hooks`.

rules.description: v0 shorthand: bare rule names contributed by the plugin. The richer structured form is `contributes.rules`.

contributes.description: VS-Code-shaped contribution surface. OPEN object: known contribution points are validated by shape; unknown ones are tolerated (forward-compat).

privileged.description: Whether the plugin requires org-admin / management privilege (e.g. the `molecule-platform` management MCP). image-gen explicitly documents `NOT privileged`. Absent/false = normal user capability.

sha256.description: Optional content digest of the published plugin artifact, for the marketplace integrity/attestation layer (Phase 3). Hex sha256.

runtimeId.description: CANONICAL runtime identifier. Hyphen form is canonical (`claude-code`); the underscore form (`claude_code`) is an accepted legacy alias that normalizes to the hyphen form. The enum is the supported runtime set plus that alias.

engines.description: Host engine constraints, like VS-Code `engines`. `molecule` pins the minimum host version as a semver range. Open object so future host engines (e.g. a runtime-specific engine) are additive.

molecule.description: Minimum Molecule host version as a semver range, e.g. `^1.0`.

contributes.description: The contribution surface. KNOWN keys (skills/hooks/rules/mcpServers/commands v1; daemons per runtime#216) below are validated by shape. UNKNOWN keys (future contribution points such as `themes`, `tabs`, `canvasElements`) are explicitly TOLERATED via additionalProperties:true — adding a new contribution point MUST NOT fail validation against this schema (forward-compat). `daemons` itself landed exactly this way: the runtime shipped it as an additive point tolerated by this open object, and this schema now captures its shape descriptively.

hooks.description: Structured hook contributions (e.g. a PreToolUse:Bash hook).

mcpServers.description: MCP server contributions. An mcpServers-shaped plugin is resolved to the MCP server adaptor for any runtime (see mcp/mcp-plugin-delivery.contract.json). The entry shape mirrors the runtime-agnostic descriptor name->{command,args?,env?}.

daemons.description: Long-running daemon contributions (ADDITIVE contribution point, captured from reality per molecule-ai-workspace-runtime#216): the workspace runtime (molecule_runtime/plugin_daemons.py) spawns each declared daemon at boot, restarts it on crash with exponential backoff (circuit breaker after 10 consecutive fast failures), and kills it with the workspace — e.g. a channel bridge like lark-channel-molecule's `lark-bridge`. The entry shape deliberately mirrors `mcpServerContribution` (name + command/args?/env?) rather than inventing a new descriptor. VALIDATION SEMANTICS — deliberately UNCONSTRAINED here, mirroring the runtime exactly: the runtime NEVER rejects a manifest over `daemons`. A non-list value, non-mapping entries, missing/blank `name`/`command`, non-string `args` items, or a non-mapping `env` are SKIPPED with a loud warning at daemon discovery (`_entry_problem`) — never a boot crash, never a manifest-validation failure. Daemons are auxiliary; a typo here must not brick the plugin at the (future fail-closed) install gate, so schema-constraining this property would CHANGE runtime semantics rather than capture them (proven: runtime test test_malformed_entries_skipped_valid_kept fails against a strict version of this property). `$defs/daemonContribution` documents the canonical WELL-FORMED entry the runtime actually spawns — the shape emitters should produce and codegen consumers should use.

anyOf.description: The canonical, well-formed form — what emitters MUST produce. Kept as a live ref so the shape stays discoverable and $defs/daemonContribution is never orphaned.

Anything else — tolerated by design: the runtime skips non-conforming values/entries at spawn discovery with a warning instead of failing manifest validation. This branch makes the anyOf always-satisfiable, which IS the contract.

path.description: Optional path to the skill content within the plugin.

event.description: Optional host event the hook binds to (e.g. `PreToolUse:Bash`).

mcpServerContribution.description: A single contributed MCP server descriptor (runtime-agnostic name->{command,args?,env?}).

env.description: Optional environment map (var->value) for the MCP server process.

privileged.description: Whether this MCP server is the privileged management surface.

daemonContribution.description: A single contributed long-running daemon, exactly the shape molecule-ai-workspace-runtime's plugin_daemons.py parses (runtime#216): `{name, command, args?, env?, cwd?}`. The runtime spawns `[command, *args]` in its own session/process group, with the workspace process env overlaid with `env`, in `cwd` (plugin-dir-relative; default: the plugin dir itself). Requiredness and item types mirror the runtime's `_entry_problem` checks verbatim: non-mapping entry, missing/whitespace-only `name` or `command`, non-string-list `args`, and non-mapping `env` are what the runtime SKIPS (with a warning) — everything else it accepts. First real consumer: lark-channel-molecule's `plugin.yaml` (`{name: lark-bridge, command: bash, args: [daemon-bootstrap.sh]}`).

name.description: Daemon name (required, non-blank — the runtime rejects a missing/empty/whitespace-only name). Namespaced by the owning plugin into the supervisor key `<plugin>/<name>`, so two plugins may both declare a daemon called `bridge`.

command.description: Launch command (required, non-blank), e.g. `bash` or `python`. The spawned argv is `[command, *args]`.

args.description: Optional command arguments. Must be a list of strings — the runtime skips the entry on any non-string item.

env.description: Optional environment map (var→value) overlaid on the workspace process env for the daemon. The runtime rejects only a non-mapping `env`; it str()-coerces non-string VALUES rather than rejecting them — string values are the canonical form. An explicitly-null `env` is treated as absent by the runtime (YAML `env:` with no value), though this schema's canonical form is to omit the key.

cwd.description: Optional working directory, resolved against the plugin dir when relative (default: the plugin dir itself). The runtime never validates `cwd` — a non-string value is str()-coerced rather than rejected; a string path is the canonical form.
